claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my daughter Alex - she's 6 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one daughter, Alex, who is 6 years old and lives with you.

To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Now I need to ask: **Are you responsible for Alex's care? For example, do you provide for her needs such as food, clothes, and other daily expenses?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for all of her care - I provide everything she needs like food, clothes, and all her daily expenses.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent, a relative, or anyone else?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One more important question:

**Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year?** (This won't affect your eligibility to receive Child Benefit, but it may mean you need to pay a tax charge on it.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16) ✓
- You live in the UK ✓
- You are responsible for Alex's care and she lives with you ✓
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her ✓
- Neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000, so you won't need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge ✓

You meet all the eligibility requirements! If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. Since neither you nor your partner has an income over £60,000, you'll receive the full benefit without any tax implications.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which is under the age threshold of 16, so she meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. The claimant is responsible for Alex's care and provides for all her needs (food, clothes, and daily expenses), which means they are responsible for the child under the rules. Alex lives with the claimant, which is one of the ways to qualify as being responsible for a child. Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which is required as only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their daughter Alex. They meet all the key requirements: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), the claimant lives in the UK, they are responsible for Alex's care and she lives with them, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her. Since neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, they will not need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

The claimant is responsible for Alex's care and provides for all her needs (food, clothes, and daily expenses), which means they are responsible for the child under the rules. Alex lives with the claimant, which is one of the ways to qualify as being responsible for a child. Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which is required as only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their daughter Alex. They meet all the key requirements: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), the claimant lives in the UK, they are responsible for Alex's care and she lives with them, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her. Since neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, they will not need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.
